qa_pairs.question: When does the annual account renewal process typically occur?
qa_pairs.answer: The annual account renewal process typically takes place in the spring.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two main purposes of the annual account renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: The main purposes are to deactivate accounts that are no longer needed and to collect information required by funding agencies and partners for reporting purposes.

qa_pairs.question: Why is collecting information about researchers and their results important?
qa_pairs.answer: This information is important for reports to funding agencies and the digital research infrastructure (DRI) ecosystem.

qa_pairs.question: How will users be notified about the account renewal process?
qa_pairs.answer: Users with an active account will receive an email during the renewal period, directing them to the account renewal form.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a user allows their CCDB account to expire?
qa_pairs.answer: If a user lets their CCDB account expire, they will no longer be able to log into any of the systems.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a Principal Investigator (PI) allows their account to expire?
qa_pairs.answer: If a PI allows their account to expire, their account as well as the accounts of all users they sponsor will be deactivated.

qa_pairs.question: How can an expired account be reactivated?
qa_pairs.answer: Expired accounts can be reactivated at any time by logging into CCDB and completing the account renewal form.

qa_pairs.question: What should a user do if they do not want to renew their CCDB account but still need to recover their files?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can request a temporary extension to recover their files by sending an email to support@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more information about account renewals?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can find more information on the Account Renewals FAQ page or by contacting renewals@tech.alliancecan.ca.
